contexts.name: Intake
contexts.description: New appeal cases: drafting, validation, submission lifecycle.

contexts.name: Legacy Tickets
contexts.description: Read-only bridge to the old ticketing system while migration is in flight. Uses the old system's vocabulary (ticket, band, zone).

contexts.name: Pricing & Fees
contexts.description: Filing fee assessment and the regulator-facing audit ledger.

contexts.name: Notifications
contexts.description: Applicant-facing messages, queued through an outbox.

terms.definition: A single appeal lodged by an applicant, from draft through decision. The canonical concept of the system.

aliases.note: Same concept, legacy vocabulary. A legacy Ticket IS a Case; ticketAdapter translates band→tier, zone→region, kind→category.

terms.definition: The post-discount, pre-levy fee figure reported to the regulator on the quarterly fee return. NOT the same as the total the applicant pays.
Applicant fee category: standard, concession, or hardship. The legacy system calls this 'band' (A/B/C).
The applicant's stated basis for the appeal. Legacy field name: 'body'.

claims.statement: applyTierDiscount() MUST run before applyRegionalAdjustment() in calculateFilingFee().
claims.why: Tribunal Fees Transparency Code (FTC Reg 4.2): tier discounts must be applied to the base filing fee before any regional levy, because the post-discount figure is the 'assessed fee' reported to the regulator on the quarterly fee return. Both steps are multiplicative, so the final total is identical in either order and the entire test suite passes both ways — the violation is only visible in the audit ledger line items the regulator reads.

provenance.source: commit 5195ade — 'Apply tier discount before regional adjustment (#47)'
provenance.author: Priya Sharma

claims.statement: 'Ticket' (legacy module) and 'Case' (intake module) are the same domain concept under two names.
claims.why: The legacy ticketing system predates the appeals service; its records are bridged nightly. Code that treats Tickets and Cases as distinct entities (e.g. counting both) double-counts appeals.

provenance.source: commit 309fd26 — 'Bridge legacy tickets into the intake pipeline (#61)'
provenance.author: Dan Okafor

claims.statement: Hardship cases are sorted ahead of the general review queue.
claims.why: Code-derived hypothesis: the comment says hardship applicants are taken first, but no trace explains whether this is policy, SLA, or developer choice.

questions.subject: redundant null check on the normalizeRef() result in ticketToCase()
questions.question: In ticketToCase(), the result of normalizeRef() is null-checked, but normalizeRef() can never return null (String.replace always returns a string). Is this guarding against something real — a planned change to normalizeRef, a known bad legacy record — or is it accidental?
questions.hypothesis: Looks intentional (explicit throw with a tailored message) but no PR, commit, or comment explains it.

routedTo.name: Dan Okafor
routedTo.basis: git blame: lines introduced in commit 309fd26 (#61), sole author of the adapter
